title: Verbena johnstonii
taxonomy: Verbena johnstonii is classified within the plant family Verbenaceae. This family, also known as the verbena or lamiaceae family, is a group of flowering plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. Verbena johnstonii belongs to the genus Verbena, which comprises approximately 250 species of plants. The genus Verbena is further divided into various sections and series based on morphological characteristics. Verbena johnstonii is likely classified as a species within one of these sections or series.

intro: Verbena johnstonii, also known as Johnston's verbena, is a plant species within the Verbena genus, which is a group of flowering plants in the verbena family (Verbenaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. The bioactive constituents of Verbena johnstonii include verbascoside, a phenylethanoid glycoside with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, as well as other compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Verbena johnstonii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
